dependencies: Dependence on small number of contract manufacturers and component suppliers
Single-source suppliers for certain types of parts
Significant portion of purchased components sourced overseas, primarily in Asia and Europe
Advanced electronic components and battery cells for energy technology products
Steel, copper and aluminum as primary raw materials
risks: Fluctuations in cost, availability, and quality of raw materials, key components and labor
Dependence on small number of outside contract manufacturers and component suppliers
Single-source suppliers for certain products and components

Risks from sourcing components in foreign countries including inflation, political and economic conditions, logistical challenges, extended container port congestion, higher logistics costs, unstable regulatory environments, changes in import and export duties, domestic and foreign customs and tariffs, currency rate fluctuations, trade restrictions, labor or civil unrest, geopolitical conflict such as in Ukraine or the Middle East, disputes with contract manufacturers or suppliers, communications challenges

Supply chain disruptions from lack of capacity, economic downturns, availability of credit, logistical challenges, labor or material shortages, trade restrictions, weather events, political instability, geopolitical conflicts, terrorism, civil unrest, disease or natural disasters

regulatory_risks: Compliance with environmental, health and safety laws and regulations including emissions to air, discharges to water, noise, generation, handling, storage, transportation, treatment, and disposal of hazardous waste
Products subject to EPA, CARB and various state and local air quality management districts regulations
Products subject to U.S. Consumer Product Safety Commission (CPSC) safety standards
Compliance with data privacy and cybersecurity regulations
CARB regulations prohibiting future sales in California of certain small off-road engines
Changes in U.S. trade policy including imposition of new or increased tariffs
Anti-dumping and countervailing duties on products or components
Compliance with U.S. Foreign Corrupt Practices Act, U.K. Bribery Act, U.N. Convention Against Bribery and local anti-corruption laws
International labor laws and work councils
Import and export control, sanction and trade restriction laws
CPSC investigation regarding compliance with reporting requirements under Consumer Product Safety Act (CPSA)
DOJ investigation regarding compliance with emissions regulations for portable generators
EPA and CARB civil document requests related to emissions compliance
Inflation Reduction Act providing funding and tax incentives for clean energy products
OECD Pillar Two Global Anti-Base Erosion model rules requiring minimum 15% tax rate
SEC climate-related disclosure requirements under Release No. 33-11275
EU Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive
Changes in government incentive or tax credit programs for clean energy products
Renewable energy mandates and investment tax credits
Production tax credits for domestic manufacturing of renewable energy products

sanction_risks: Changes in U.S. trade policy including renegotiating and terminating bilateral or multi-lateral trade agreements
Tariffs on foreign goods from various countries and regions, most notably China
Foreign governments adopting responsive trade policies making it more difficult or costly to do business or import products or components
Geopolitical conflict such as conflicts in Ukraine or the Middle East affecting supply chain
